value.title: APMA Clean Architecture boundaries (non-negotiable)
value.content:
APMA follows Clean Architecture with exactly four projects: Api, Application, Domain, Infrastructure.

Rules:
- Agent implementations live in Infrastructure (Apma.Infrastructure/Agents).
- Agent interfaces and output models live in Application.
- Workflows and gates are Application-layer concerns.
- Do not create a separate Apma.Agents project.

RAG guidance:
- Do NOT implement a separate Context Retriever agent.
- Generator agents integrate RAG via AIContextProviderFactory with TextSearchProvider.

Naming and responsibilities:
- Infrastructure integrates external services (Foundry, Search, DevOps).
- Application orchestrates and validates; it depends on abstractions, not implementations.

value.title: RAG quality standards for APMA outputs
value.content:
RAG content must help the model produce enterprise-ready work items. Prefer concise, reusable standards and high-signal examples.

Golden examples should demonstrate:
- Specific titles (no generic placeholders; avoid type prefixes like 'Epic:' or 'PBI:')
- Descriptions with clear scope (in/out), measurable outcomes, dependencies, and risks
- Acceptance criteria phrased in Given/When/Then
- Definition of Done that includes tests, telemetry, security review, and documentation

Templates should provide repeatable structure and placeholders.

When retrieval returns no results, generation must still avoid placeholder text and meet minimum quality thresholds.

value.title: Work item writing standards (enterprise Agile)
value.content:
Title standards:
- Use a verb + object + qualifier (e.g., 'Enable RBAC for publish operations')
- Minimum 10 characters; avoid generic titles ('new feature', 'update')
- Do not prefix with work item type

Description standards:
- Minimum 50 characters with meaningful context
- Include: Summary, Context, Scope, NFRs, Acceptance Criteria, DoD
- Avoid placeholder text (TBD/TODO)

Acceptance criteria standards:
- Use Given/When/Then
- Make outcomes observable and testable
- PBIs should have 2+ criteria

Definition of Done baseline:
- Code reviewed, tests added, telemetry added, no secrets, docs updated.

value.title: Security baseline for APMA services
value.content:
Security baseline:
- Prefer secretless authentication using Managed Identity in deployed environments
- Store secrets (if required) in Key Vault; never hardcode in code or config
- Apply least privilege via RBAC (scoped roles, minimal permissions)
- Log security-relevant events (authorization failures, publish attempts, admin actions)

Privacy and data handling:
- Do not log prompt content containing sensitive data
- Use correlation IDs for tracing without leaking user content

Operational controls:
- Rate-limit or backoff on 429s from AI services
- Validate inputs and sanitize outputs before persistence.

value.title: Observability requirements for generation workflows
value.content:
Observability requirements:
- Use OpenTelemetry for traces, metrics, and logs
- Propagate a correlationId across API request, workflow steps, search retrieval, and AI invocations
- Capture and log diagnostics when external calls fail (without secrets)

Recommended telemetry:
- Counters: generation_requests_total by workItemType and outcome
- Histograms: generation_latency_ms by stage (retrieve, generate, validate)
- Traces: spans for SearchAsync and agent RunAsync

Operational readiness:
- Provide a runbook for common failures (429 throttling, search outage, auth failures)

value.title: GCC High considerations for APMA deployments
value.content:
GCC High requirements:
- Support .azure.us endpoints for Foundry project inference
- Use AzureAuthorityHosts.AzureGovernment when IsGccHigh is true
- Token scope differs: https://cognitiveservices.azure.us/.default

Constraints:
- Use non-hosted agents; do not use hosted/persistent agent APIs
- Avoid hardcoded .azure.com endpoints

Validation:
- Configuration must be environment-driven (IsGccHigh flag)
- Connectivity and auth should work with Managed Identity.

value.title: Azure DevOps publishing standards and traceability
value.content:
Publishing standards:
- Always support preview before publish (diff view and hierarchy)
- Record traceability: draftId → adoWorkItemId mapping with correlationId
- Use least privilege identity for publish operations

Work item linkage:
- Maintain hierarchy (Epic → Feature → PBI)
- Capture URLs/links for created items

Operational guidance:
- If publishing partially fails, produce a recovery plan listing what was created and what remains
- Provide rollback guidance (tags, deletion strategy, or revert steps).

value.title: Testing strategy for APMA (unit, integration, E2E)
value.content:
Testing strategy:
- Unit tests validate domain behaviors, gates, and workflow decisions
- Integration tests validate RAG retrieval integration and agent wiring (mock where needed)
- E2E tests validate UI flow (create session → generate → review → publish preview)

Principles:
- Prefer deterministic tests (fixed seed data and stable fixtures)
- Avoid network dependencies in unit tests
- For integrations, verify graceful degradation when dependencies are down.

value.title: Resilience patterns (retries, timeouts, graceful degradation)
value.content:
Resilience patterns:
- Honor Retry-After on 429 from AI services
- Use exponential backoff with jitter and max delay
- Use timeouts and cancellation tokens for all outbound calls
- Degrade gracefully when context retrieval fails (generate without RAG but log warning)

Logging:
- Include dependency name, status code, retry count, and correlationId
- Never log secrets or full prompt content.
